$comment: Contract for the structured arrays + triage_summary in Diagnostic_State.meta.json. items.$schema_ref names the per-element schema each array must satisfy (a stdlib extension to JSON Schema, read by structured_findings.py). The validator additionally enforces the cross-field rule — a non-empty findings[] requires a triage_summary whose counts equal the findings[] severity tally — which plain schema cannot express. Other sidecar fields are permitted (additionalProperties true).
title: APODICTIC diagnostic-state sidecar (apodictic.diagnostic-state.v1)

complexity_signals.$comment: Adaptive Mid-Run Mode Escalation (docs/adaptive-mode-escalation.md): Tier-1 complexity signals Pass 0/1 record so `validate.sh escalation-check` can decide whether to recommend escalating the execution mode before Tier 2. tier1_finding_count is NOT stored — escalation-check computes it from the ledger's F-P0-/F-P1- blocks.

execution.$comment: Runner-Governed Execution state. Increment 5 (state_version 2): gate_events[] is the canonical append-only gate-decision log; phase/allowed_next/pending_gate/finding_states are a recomputable resume pointer (== fold(gate_events), asserted by run_gate.py --check-state). gates (per-phase result map) is DEPRECATED — read only when state_version < 2 (legacy fallback).

state_version.$comment: 2 = gate_events model; absent/1 = legacy gates-map sidecar. Readers branch on this.

gate_events.$comment: append-only gate-decision log. Nested under execution, so validate_sidecar_obj's top-level walker does not reach it; gate-state (run_gate.py --check-state) iterates and validates per item.

pending_gate.$comment: the gate /start must resolve next (earliest open exception); OMITTED when none (never null).

gates.$comment: DEPRECATED by gate_events; read only when state_version < 2.

finding_states.$comment: finding_id -> lifecycle state (locked | delivered | revised); advanced forward-only by the gate.

attested.$comment: DEPRECATED by gate_events[].attested_items; legacy phase -> confirmed-items map.
